comparison.method: Newest article selected by article date from the authenticated category page. July 6 watchlist/monitoring contained NTAP only. July 13 introduces MU, ALAB and SPCX as current shakeout-monitoring examples, so all three are new_watchlist. NTAP moved to a disclosed position. There are no continued_watchlist or rejoined_watchlist names.

sourceNotes: Authenticated Chrome/Kajabi session verified the July 13, 2026 article as newest by article date, ahead of July 6, 2026.

Included tickers only when visible in the July 13 body text, financial disclosure, body chart images, or embedded holdings table. Sidebar lessons, previous/next previews, comments, thumbnails and unrelated page assets were excluded.
Weekly new watchlist: MU, ALAB and SPCX. MRVL appears among the shakeout examples but is classified only as a current position because the same-day financial disclosure explicitly lists it long.
Current positions disclosed: MRVL, PANW, CPER, DAVE, DELL, LRCX, KLAC, HOOD and NTAP. ONTO is exited. CPER is retained as disclosed exposure but excluded from the normal US single-stock screen.

LRCX evidence conflicts inside the current article: disclosure/prose imply a live position and a future 50MA exit rule, while the embedded holdings table says Sold. It is classified as position with a manual-verification caveat.

Nasdaq daily OHLCV was refreshed through the latest confirmed regular-session close, July 13, 2026, for all listed equities plus SPY. Six-month stock-versus-SPY performance is a price-relative proxy, not a proprietary RS rank.
Trend -> Base/Pattern -> Pivot -> Volume -> Risk and the Bottom-Up Sell Techniques are applied. If any core execution element is unclear, the action remains wait/watchlist rather than buy.
Educational trade-plan candidates only, not personalized financial advice.

candidates.articleEvidence: MU is one of the current shakeout examples shown immediately after the author describes looking for 20-30% corrections into important support followed by a fast reversal.
candidates.chartEvidence: Body chart shows MU correcting from roughly 1,240 to the 886.55 support/50MA area, then rebounding; July 13 closed 937.00 above the rising 50MA but below the 10/20MA resistance cluster near 1,000-1,050.
candidates.caveat: Conditional plan only. The entry is below the short-MA resistance cluster; if price cannot reclaim that cluster quickly, remain watchlist-only.
candidates.comment: MU: new_watchlist. MU is one of the current shakeout examples shown immediately after the author describes looking for 20-30% corrections into important support followed by a fast reversal. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.

candidates.articleEvidence: ALAB is a current shakeout example shown inside the article body; the chart annotation identifies AI data-centre connectivity and memory-bottleneck exposure.
candidates.chartEvidence: Body chart marks support near 366.67 and deeper support near 295.09. July 13 sold off on 6.29M shares and closed 362.05 below the first shelf, while remaining above rising 50/150/200MA.
candidates.caveat: The mathematical plan reaches 2R, but current pullback quality is poor because support failed on elevated selling. Wait for proof before treating 380 as actionable.
candidates.comment: ALAB: new_watchlist. ALAB is a current shakeout example shown inside the article body; the chart annotation identifies AI data-centre connectivity and memory-bottleneck exposure. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.

candidates.chartEvidence: Body chart shows a return to the original breakout shelf and rising 50MA around 330; July 13 closed 329.92, just below the calculated 50MA 330.91.
candidates.caveat: Same-article conflict: disclosure/prose imply a live position, while holdings table says Sold. Classified as position because the dated disclosure and explicit forward-looking exit rule are more specific; verify manually before acting.
candidates.comment: LRCX: position. Disclosure lists LRCX long and prose says the author will monitor for a rebound and cut only on a close below 50MA. The embedded table also says Sold, creating an unresolved same-article conflict. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.

candidates.articleEvidence: Financial disclosure lists NTAP long; holdings table says Added. Article says it broke out July 9 and the slight pullback is constructive.
candidates.chartEvidence: Body chart shows the descending flag breakout and a retest around 163-164. July 13 closed 163.93 above rising 50/150/200MA but below the July 9-10 highs.
candidates.caveat: NTAP was July 6 new watchlist but is a disclosed July 13 position, so it is excluded from the weekly new/continued watchlist buckets.
candidates.comment: NTAP: position. Financial disclosure lists NTAP long; holdings table says Added. Article says it broke out July 9 and the slight pullback is constructive. Educational trade-plan candidate only, not personalized financial advice.
